items.strictMarriageDomain: For sets of men $M$ and women $W$ with utilities $v_m:M\to W\to\mathbb{R}$ and $v_w:W\to M\to\mathbb{R}$, the strict marriage domain is the condition that men's preferences are strict, women's preferences are strict, and every man-woman pair is acceptable.
items.stableMarriage: An assignment $\mu$ is stable for utilities $v_m,v_w$ if every man and every woman weakly prefers their assigned match to being unmatched, and there is no pair $(m,w)$ such that $m$ strictly prefers $w$ to $\mu(m)$ and $w$ strictly prefers $m$ to $\mu(w)$.
items.completeMarriage: An assignment $\mu$ is complete if every man is matched to some woman and every woman is matched to some man.
items.applicantOptimalStableMarriage: A matching $\mu$ is applicant-optimal stable if it is stable and, for every stable matching $\mu'$, every applicant/man weakly prefers their match under $\mu$ to their match under $\mu'$.
items.theorem1_stable_marriage_exists: For finite sets $M$ and $W$ with decidable equality, if $|M|=|W|$ and utilities $v_m,v_w$ satisfy the strict marriage domain, then there exists an assignment $\mu$ that is stable and complete.
items.college_admissions_stable_assignment_exi: For finite applicant and college sets with decidable equality, any college quotas and applicant/college utilities admit an assignment $\mu$ that is stable for the many-to-one college-admissions problem.
items.theorem2_applicant_optimality: For finite applicant and college sets with decidable equality, if the two sides have equal cardinality and the utilities satisfy the strict marriage domain, then deferred acceptance produces a complete matching that is applicant-optimal among stable matchings.
